summary: The `pnpm audit` command detected a moderate severity vulnerability in the `ip-address` package (versions <=10.1.0), which has an XSS flaw in Address6 HTML-emitting methods. The audit exited with code 1, failing the CI job.
root_cause: A known security vulnerability (GHSA-v2v4-37r5-5v8g) exists in the transitive dependency `ip-address` (<=10.1.0) reachable via `network__fetch > @pnpm/network.agent > @pnpm/network.proxy-agent > socks-proxy-agent > socks > ip-address`. The `pnpm audit` step fails with exit code 1 when any vulnerability is found.

evidence.quote: │ moderate            │ ip-address has XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods   │
evidence.reason: Identifies the specific vulnerability detected by pnpm audit — a moderate-severity XSS issue in the ip-address package.
Shows the currently installed version of ip-address is within the vulnerable range.
Shows the dependency chain through which the vulnerable package is introduced.
Confirms pnpm audit found exactly one vulnerability, causing a non-zero exit.
Confirms the CI step failed due to the audit finding a vulnerability.

suggested_fix: Update the `ip-address` transitive dependency to >=10.1.1 (the patched version per the audit output). This can be done by updating the direct dependency `socks` or `socks-proxy-agent` to a version that pulls in ip-address >=10.1.1, or by adding an override/resolution in the root `package.json` to force ip-address to >=10.1.1.
